title: bromopsis inermis subsp. pumpelliana
intro: Bromopsis inermis subsp. pumpelliana, also known as Mountain Brome, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The Mountain Brome is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
